predictions.rationale: Market is emphatic: Switzerland at 79% against a Qatar side out of its depth at this level. The Swiss carry the deeper, more proven squad and Qatar's home-tournament status doesn't move a line this lopsided.

Market favours Brazil (56%) but the edge is the thinnest of any Brazil fixture this tournament, and it drifted toward Morocco (58→56) over the last day. Brazil are without right-back Wesley (out of the tournament) and have Neymar a game-time doubt with a calf issue, while Morocco — 2022 semi-finalists — field Hakimi, Brahim Diaz and Bilal El Khannouss despite losing Aguerd and Ezzalzouli to injury. This is a genuine banana-skin: I keep the narrow Brazil call but at low confidence.
predictions.disagreement: Several tipsters lean to Morocco +draw or the draw outright given Brazil's defensive absences; the 56% may overrate a Selecao missing its first-choice right-back.

sources.title: Sports Mole — Brazil vs Morocco team news & predicted XIs

predictions.rationale: Market backs Scotland at 62% and the gap to Haiti's 16% is decisive. Scotland carry injury problems — Gilmour, Souttar, Christie and Doak are all out — which thins the squad, but the quality difference over Haiti still holds the away call comfortably.
Market makes Turkey clear favourites (56%) against Australia's 19%. Turkey's individual quality through the spine outweighs the Socceroos' organisation and the line gives no reason to deviate.
As one-sided as the board gets: Germany at 92% against World Cup debutants Curaçao. Expect a comfortable, high-margin home win barring a bizarre collapse.

Market shades the Netherlands (48%) over a live Japan (25%) with the draw at 27% — close to a pick'em. The Dutch have a first-choice keeper doubt (Verbruggen, bruised hip) and Depay short of fitness, while Japan are without retired captain Endo and the injured Mitoma. I keep the narrow Oranje lean but this is a coin-flip with real draw equity.
predictions.disagreement: Tipsters split; Japan's pace on the break makes the draw and an upset both live, so the 48% is not a strong steer.

sources.title: Sports Mole — Netherlands vs Japan team news
Squawka — Netherlands vs Japan predicted lineups

predictions.rationale: Rare market where the away side leads (Ecuador 40%) ahead of the draw (34%) and Ivory Coast (26%) — a tight, low-scoring profile. Ecuador's miserly back line (Pacho, Hincapie, Torres) points to few goals, but captain Enner Valencia is a minor fitness doubt, while Ivory Coast miss Ndicka. With both defences strong and the spread so compressed, I take the draw.
predictions.disagreement: Market favourite is Ecuador, so the draw call is a mild deviation toward the highest-probability low-scoring outcome.

sources.title: Sports Mole — Ivory Coast vs Ecuador preview

predictions.rationale: Market gives Sweden the edge (50%) over Tunisia (22%) with a sizeable draw share (28%). Sweden's attacking talent carries the home lean, though Tunisia's defensive discipline keeps it from being a blowout.

Market has Spain at 88% and the news is positive: Lamine Yamal and Nico Williams are on track to be available after fitness scares. World Cup debutants Cape Verde will sit deep, but Spain's quality should tell comfortably.

sources.title: Al Jazeera — Yamal, Williams available for opener

predictions.rationale: Market favours Belgium (59%) with a full-strength side — De Bruyne and Lukaku both fit. The watch-out is Egypt's defensive record (zero conceded in six qualifiers) and Salah on the break, which caps confidence; I take Belgium but respect the under and a possible low-scoring grind.
predictions.disagreement: Egypt's compact block and clean-sheet record make the draw a live tipster play against the 59% line.

sources.title: Squawka — Belgium vs Egypt team news & lineups

predictions.rationale: Market is firm on Uruguay (67%) against Saudi Arabia (12%). Uruguay's spine — Araujo, Gimenez, Valverde, Nunez — is far stronger, and Saudi Arabia's late coaching change (Renard out, Donis in) adds cohesion risk. Comfortable away lean.

sources.title: Squawka — Saudi Arabia vs Uruguay team news

predictions.rationale: Market shades Iran (52%) over New Zealand (20%) with a heavy draw share (27%). Iran's organisation and tournament experience carry a narrow home lean in what projects as a tight, low-scoring game.

Market makes France clear favourites (65%) and the team news helps: Saliba is fit and Dembele is set to return. Senegal are a serious side, so I trim confidence, but France's depth and attacking quality hold the home call.
predictions.disagreement: Senegal's physical midfield can trouble France; tipsters flag the draw as value against a 65% that may price France's class a touch high in an opener.

sources.title: FOX Sports — Saliba fit, Dembele to return

predictions.rationale: Market is decisive for Norway (80%) against Iraq (7%). With Haaland leading the line Norway have a class gap that the line fully reflects; comfortable away pick.
Market backs Argentina strongly (69%) with Messi expected to start his final World Cup. The holders' quality dwarfs Algeria's; only a cagey opener tempers the confidence.

sources.title: Bolavip — Argentina predicted lineup with Messi

predictions.rationale: Market makes Austria heavy favourites (72%) against World Cup debutants Jordan. Austria's European pedigree and squad depth carry a comfortable home call.

Market favours Portugal heavily (74%). Ronaldo starts if fit and the squad is loaded, though Rafael Leao's warm-up red card may keep him out of this opener. Even rotated, Portugal's depth should see this through comfortably.

sources.title: Tips.GG — Portugal vs DR Congo predicted lineups

predictions.rationale: Market shades England (55%) over a veteran Croatia (19%) with the draw at 25%. Saka is a fitness doubt (achilles), which thins England's right side, but Tuchel's side still has the stronger spine; I keep the home lean at moderate confidence.
predictions.disagreement: Croatia's experienced midfield (Modric-era core) can control tempo and make the draw a live tipster angle against England in an opener.

predictions.rationale: Tight market: Ghana 44%, draw 28%, Panama 28% — barely separated. Ghana's athletic forward line gives a slim home edge, but Panama are organised and competitive, so this is a low-confidence lean.
predictions.disagreement: With Ghana under 45% several tipsters see the draw or Panama as value; treat the home call cautiously.

predictions.rationale: Market is firm on Colombia (69%) against debutants Uzbekistan (11%). Colombia's quality through James and the front line is a clear gap; comfortable away pick.
Market shades the Czechs (54%) over South Africa (20%) with the draw at 25%. Czech set-piece threat and physicality give a moderate home lean, but South Africa's pace keeps it from being comfortable.
Market favours Switzerland (60%) against Bosnia (16%). The Swiss carry the more balanced side; Bosnia's Dzeko-led attack can threaten but the home lean is solid.
Market backs Canada heavily (73%) at home against Qatar (9%). Alphonso Davies was targeting this game for his return from a hamstring issue, which only strengthens an already-clear edge.

Close market: Mexico 48%, draw 29%, South Korea 24%. Home crowd and Gimenez's movement give Mexico a slim edge, but Son Heung-min makes South Korea dangerous and the draw is well-priced. Low-confidence home lean.
predictions.disagreement: Both sides likely won their openers, so a cagey draw to protect seeding is a live tipster angle against the sub-50% favourite.

sources.title: Squawka — Mexico vs South Korea preview

predictions.rationale: Market favours the hosts USA (60%) over Australia (17%) with home-crowd backing after a strong opener. The USMNT's attacking depth carries the lean; Australia's organisation can keep it competitive but not even.

sources.title: CBS Sports — World Cup group standings & results

predictions.rationale: Market makes Morocco favourites (48%) over Scotland (23%) with the draw at 29%. Morocco's individual quality (Hakimi, Brahim Diaz) carries the away lean, but this is their second game in a tight turnaround after Brazil and rotation/fatigue is a real factor; Scotland are also missing several injured players. Low-confidence away call.
predictions.disagreement: If Morocco rest starters after the Brazil game, the draw and a Scotland upset both become live; the 48% may not price rotation.

predictions.rationale: Market is emphatic: Brazil at 89% against Haiti (3%). Even with rotation Brazil's attacking quality overwhelms Haiti; expect a comfortable, high-margin home win.

Tight market: Turkey 46%, draw 29%, Paraguay 24%. Turkey's attacking talent gives a slim edge, but Paraguay are well-drilled and hard to break down, so this is a cautious home lean with real draw equity.
predictions.disagreement: With Turkey under 47% the draw is a live tipster play against a disciplined Paraguay side.
